facade_colour: black-painted storefront and lower wall beneath a blue-green mural-covered gabled upper facade

hcd_data.architectural_style: Converted House-form, Detached (confirmed – HCD Plan Vol. 2)
hcd_data.statement_of_contribution: The property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that has been modified to accommodate a commercial conversion through ground floor alterations. Through its construction date and typology, the property is associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s. With its visibly layered built form, the property is also associated with the patterns of commercial adaptation and expansion that began in the early twentieth century. Having housed commercial uses at the ground floor since 1889, the property has retained certain building features including its gabled roof and second-storey window openings. Anchoring the northeast corner of Augusta Avenue and Nassau Street, the property supports and maintains the historic mixed use character of the area and is physically, functionally, visually, and historically linked to the surrounding market area. With storefronts that address both street facing elevations, the property defines the intersection of Augusta Avenue and Nassau Street.

context.street_character: One-way NB | 30 km/h | Sub-standard sidewalks | Primary commercial spine | Pedestrian Sunday route

photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Facade is occupied and readable from the street, with contemporary storefront alterations layered onto the historic building form.
photo_observations.cornice_details: Simple fascia and coping with limited historic trim visible.
photo_observations.chimney_details: No chimney clearly visible from the selected frontage view.
photo_observations.decorative_details: Decorative character is primarily expressed through the storefront treatment and surviving upper-storey massing.
photo_observations.storefront_description: Dark corner storefront with mural art and many small display panes.
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Storefront and signage have been modernized from the original historic condition.
photo_observations.notes: A geocoordinate-labelled photo in this batch appears to show the same building and was treated as a duplicate alias.

photo_observations.window_details: Two second-floor windows sit within the mural-clad gable while the ground floor is broken into small storefront panes and a corner entry.
photo_observations.door_details: Corner glazed entry at Augusta and the side street.
photo_observations.signage_observed: Turning Point of Seasons lettering and neighboring market signage visible near the corner.
photo_observations.batch_008_note: Side view from Nassau St captured at night in batch_008. Limited additional detail visible. Confidence 0.3 for this observation angle.

facade_description: 257 Augusta Ave presents a converted house-form, detached facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a gable, consistent with its converted house-form, detached typology. The facade reads primarily as mixed in a black-painted storefront and lower wall beneath a blue-green mural-covered gabled upper facade palette, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 4, 2 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 1 principal entrance. Character-defining facade elements include cornice. HCD note: The property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that has been modified to accommodate a commercial conversion through ground floor alterations. Through its construction date and typology, the property is associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s. With its visibly layered built form, the property is also associated with the patterns of commercial adaptation and expansion that began in the early twentieth century. Having housed commercial uses at the ground floor since 1889, the property has retained certain building features including its gabled roof and second-storey window openings. Anchoring the northeast corner of Augusta Avenue and Nassau Street, the property supports and maintains the historic mixed use character of the area and is physically, functionally, visually, and historically linked to the surrounding market area. With storefronts that address both street facing elevations, the property defines the intersection of Augusta Avenue and Nassau Street.
heritage_summary: With storefronts that address both street facing elevations, the property defines the intersection of Augusta Avenue and Nassau Street. The property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that has been modified to accommodate a commercial conversion through ground floor alterations.
